title: Chapter 24
text: Chapter Twenty - One L ara stared at Ronin’s ruined flesh. Pulling the pieces of metal out of his casing had been like plucking out splinters, and she’d done that more times than she could count for herself and Tabitha. But this … This was skin ! Even if it wasn’t living flesh, it was a part of Ronin…and he wanted her to cut it off. She bit her lower lip, looking up to meet his steady, one- eyed gaze . “Does it hurt?” she asked . “No. Not anymore.” He turned and swung his legs over the side of the bed. “I can take care of it .” She placed a hand on his chest. He froze. It was eerie, how suddenly and completely his movement halted . “I’ll do it, Ronin. Just…tell me what I need to do.” Her hand fell away from his chest . Slowly, he turned his head, eye dipping to follow her hand. “You’ve done enough already .” “God dammit, Ronin!” She sprung off the bed and threw her arms to the side. “I said I’d do it. It’s the least I can do after everything you’ve done for me .” Lara collected the tools from the chest, unsure of which she’d need, and spread them atop the bed . “We’ll get your back first .” He was quiet for a long while, his lip-plates pressed together. Just as she wondered if he’d shut down, he twisted and picked up a knife, staring down at it . “Use this to cut around my waist. An inch or two below the scorch marks.” He deftly spun the knife on his palm, holding the grip to her . “Okay.” She wrapped her fingers around the handle. The knife was impossibly heavy . Ronin turned away and stood, hooking his thumbs on the waist of his pants to tug them down below the burn marks. While his front had been devastated, his back was damaged mainly near his shoulders and at his sides . Sitting on the edge of the bed, Lara raised the knife, stilling the blade just before it touched his skin. Her stomach churned. She’d never cut anyone, apart from the accident with Tabitha. Now she was about to cut into the only other person she cared about . He said it doesn’t hurt . She had to trust his word. Inhaling deeply, she pressed the blade to his skin. The pressure it took to finally break through nearly killed her nerve, but she pushed on . For him . It was an agonizing process. The give of his skin was contrasted by the solid, often uneven metal beneath, and she wasn’t sure if she should be more concerned about damaging his casing or the knife. She cut up from his hips to his armpits and then, wishing she could close her eyes, worked her fingers beneath his skin. It peeled with surprising ease, revealing the intricate metal plates beneath . She shuddered inwardly as some of the burned portions flaked apart in her fingers, but continued her work. After placing the strips of skin on the floor, she shifted her attention to his arms, picking away scorched shreds of skin and cloth. Ronin remained silent, throughout . Lara glanced up at his face when she moved to his front. His gaze was focused straight ahead. Had he withdrawn from her again ? She repeated the process, slicing his flesh over the waist of his pants, gently peeling and scraping away everything above. The pile on the floor grew. She made sure not to look at it, unable to guess what her reaction to it would be . When she was finally done, she stepped back and looked him over. Below the waist, he was human. The rest of him was something else entirely . His metal plates mimicked human muscles — the curve of powerful shoulders and biceps, ridges on his torso like pecs and abs. None of the lines were quite the same, without skin to finish them out, but his parts came together with an elegance she’d never seen . “Done?” he asked, jarring her from her thoughts . “I think so .” “No more overload .” “You turned it back on already ?” “Doesn’t take long .” She trailed her fingers along the edge of skin at his hip. “You feel this ?” A moment’s hesitation . “ Yes .” “Does it hurt ?” “No.” Had his voice softened ? “Good.” Lara lowered her hand. “ Anything else ?” Ronin shook his head. “I’m going to the Clinic for repairs. You should get some sleep .” “Wait.” She gathered the tools from the bed. “Stay. Wait till morning. It’s only a few hours, and I can scrub the scorch marks from your casing .” “It will be more bearable to you — ” “Get on the fucking bed and lay down.” She fixed him with a stern look before dumping the tools on the chest and stalking to the bathroom . Why was it so difficult for him to get it through his thick metal skull? It didn’t matter . So what if he was made of metal and plastic, powered by electric cells instead of food and water? He had a mind, had his own thoughts, his own opinions. Were he still covered in flesh, she’d have a hard time guessing he was a bot. Only his bare hands had given him away when they first met . Lara took the bucket from the little storage room and filled it with hot water from the tub. Grabbing a handcloth, she carried the bucket into the bedroom . Ronin lay upon the bed, as she had commanded, his boots on the floor beside it . Placing the bucket in the crook of his arm, she climbed onto the bed and straddled his waist. He stared up at her, but she refused to meet his gaze as she dunked the cloth in the water, wrung out the excess, and scrubbed the soot from his brow . Even after all they’d shared, he still thought she saw him that way ? She ran the cloth down his cheeks and nose, along his jawline, over his lip-plates, pressing a little harder with each passing moment. When she rinsed the cloth, the water turned black. She squeezed the moisture out with enough force to make her hands ache . Jaw clenched, she washed his neck and chest . “Why?” he asked . She looked into his eye, her brows falling. He still had to ask that? “What do you fucking mean, why?” The bed creaked as she scrubbed his torso . “I’m a bot .” Lara hit him. Her fist connected with one of his chest plates, and she hissed, knuckles throbbing immediately . Ronin gently took her hand in his and turned it to see the drop of blood welling through her broken skin. His lip-plates drooped . “Because of this !” She pulled her hand from his, leaned forward, and cupped his face. “Because you care. You care for me, and I wouldn’t have waited here if I didn’t care for you , too .” “After all the things my kind has done to you…to all humans …” “You aren’t your kind, Ronin.” Sitting back on his thighs, she slid her hands down to the center of his chest. “You’re unique. I’m almost convinced you have a heart in there. But you prove feelings don’t come from there, anyway. I’ve known plenty of people who had hearts and didn’t feel anything half as strongly as you do .” He placed his hands over hers. “ I feel .” “So do I. For you.” She slipped one hand free and trailed it lower, over the ridges of his abdomen. “Whether you’re metal—” her fingertips teased the edges of his skin “—or flesh .” She dropped her gaze. His cock was swelling in his pants. It was amazing to her how close that part of his anatomy was to a human’s, how it responded so naturally to her touch. She grasped the top button of his pants, but he stopped her with his hand . There was hesitance in his eye. Insecurity. He said nothing, and didn’t resist when she moved his arm aside . “Let me.” Unfastening his pants, she drew them down. His cock sprang free, thick and long. She wrapped her fingers around it. “Do you feel this ?” Ronin’s mouth parted, brow plate dropping. He nodded and ran his warm, solid palms over her knees . Without breaking eye contact, she stroked her fist along his length, and he hardened further. Lara rose to her knees, positioning herself over him, and guided the tip of his cock to the entrance of her sex. Slowly, she lowered herself, taking him inch-by-inch. His hands moved to her ass, thumbs hooked between her thighs and hips . “Do you feel this?” she asked again, leaning forward to press her palms to the warm plates of his chest. Her hair cascaded around her . “I feel you .” His voice was whisper - soft . His touch and the huskiness of his words coaxed moisture from her, easing his entry as she lifted and dropped her hips. The slide of his shaft ignited intense heat within her. Before long, it would be an inferno . She resisted the urge to close her eyes and kept her gaze locked with his, dipping her head closer to him . “Do you really feel me?” Her lips were a breath away from his . Ronin cupped the nape of her neck. “All of you, Lara Brooks .” “Do you feel how hot I am? How wet I am, for you ?” He nodded, his pelvis moving in time with hers . “I see this. I see you. And,” she brushed her lips over his, “I want you .” Slipping his fingers into her hair, he drew her into a full kiss. His lip-plates were solid, but yielded to the pressure, parting when his tongue sought entry to her mouth. She opened to him. His tongue was dry, with a slight, bumpy texture, and she recalled the feel of it between her legs as she slid hers against it. That spark of memory heightened her pleasure. It was his cock inside her now, thrusting, stroking that place beyond her reach . She moaned against his mouth, overcome with the building sensations sweeping through her. She spread her legs wider, wanting more of him. Her skin tingled, and she felt like she was floating; only Ronin’s touch grounded her, only his solidness kept her from drifting away . Moving her hands to the bedding, she clenched the sheets. “Ronin,” she rasped, craving the bliss only he could deliver. She leaned her head on his shoulder . Ronin’s hands fell to her ass, and he took over the rhythm. His thrusts quickened as he lifted and pulled her down on him, tilting her hips just right. Each stroke from this angle sent waves of ecstasy through her, each stronger than the last, until they consumed her. Lara’s body tightened and she cried out. Star-like sparks danced behind her closed eyelids . Ronin’s fingertips were white-hot where they pressed into her skin. He stiffened beneath her, cock pulsing against her inner walls . When she finally opened her eyes again, she lay upon his chest, his arms around her. One of his hands was beneath her shirt, fingers brushing the small of her back. His shaft filled her, still hard and hot . She ran her pointer finger over one of the damaged spots on his chest, moving from smooth metal to jagged and back again . “I don’t know what I am anymore,” he said, “and I don’t think I care, as long as I have you .” “I love you.” The words spilled out, unbidden, but they sounded right . Lara tilted her head and looked up at him. “Never thought I’d say that to anyone but Tabitha, but it’s true.” She curled her palm along his jawline. “I don’t know when it happened. I think, maybe, it’s when I said you weren’t human .” “You fell in love with me when you insulted me?” he asked, voice wavering . Lara smirked. “Yeah. Kinda funny, when you think about it , huh ?” His lips shifted, plates beneath his cheeks moving up and back. A smile. “You do have an infuriating-but-charming air about you .” Laughing, she lifted her head to look down at him, pulling her hair out of her face. “I was attracted to you way earlier than that, but I fought it like hell .” “I was attracted to you from the first moment I saw you, Lara Brooks .” Her smile faded, and she tilted her head. “You wanted me for more than dancing, didn’t you ?” “I don’t know,” he said after a while. “Yes? It wasn’t a logical decision, just…pure desire. I don’t understand what I felt then, and I still don’t understand it now .” “Why me? Why not a different human? Someone easier to get along with, maybe ?” “Everyone else moves the same way, whether metal or flesh and bone. Lifelessly. Dull-eyed.” His hand trailed up her spine. “You’re the first person I’ve seen who moves like you’re alive , with a fire inside you .” “I didn’t feel it at the time.” The day or two during which they’d met had been amongst the lowest of her life. If he hadn’t come, after she lost the ring…would her despair have won out ? “But you did. You always do. The pain and sadness…it’s part of living, isn’t it? Being numb to those emotions is what makes us feel lifeless .” “And you do feel, Ronin. You’re just as alive as I am .” “Because of you .” “I might bring it out of you, a little,” she replied, grinning . “You bring something out of me, certainly .” “Hmm.” Lara wriggled her hips. “I do, for sure. Out of you, and into me .” “I’d say I’ve come to like humans, but it may only be you .” She slapped his shoulder. “Better be only me .” Ronin rose, flipping her onto her back without withdrawing from her. Lara shrieked with laughter as he propped himself on his elbows and a knee. She wrapped her legs around his waist, drawing herself snugly against him . “There is only you .” His sincerity was clear. She pressed her lips to his. They moved as one, her hands exploring the altered landscape of his body. Only sensation remained; there was no room for words, no need . * * * R onin waited until Lara was sound asleep before he exited the bed. Despite his care, the springs and frame creaked with his movement . She stirred, lashes lifting, and peered at him with groggy eyes. Her fingers loosely closed around his forearm . “Need to go to the Clinic,” he said. “The sooner I’m repaired, the sooner we can leave .” “Okay.” Her eyelids fluttered shut. “I’ll be… waiting .” Her grip, already weak, slackened, and her hand slipped off his arm. It trailed heat in its wake. Gradually, her breathing evened out. Even now, knowing how important it was to repair the damage he’d sustained, it was difficult to leave her . Leaning down, he pulled on his boots, lacing them loosely. They’d be off again soon enough. He stood, easing his weight onto his right leg to test the strength of his knee. Diagnostics assessed its current state . Partial mobility restored. Actuator operating at 32% functionality, with 140% of normal power draw. Power leak probable . Though his pants were damaged, too, he pulled them up and fastened them. His steps were slow as he crossed the room to the closet. Each time he shifted his weight onto his right leg, his knee buckled, but the joint locks initiated before it collapsed. Not ideal, but he didn’t have far to go. Just another mile . He selected a clean pair of pants and found the shirt Lara had made for him, folding both articles of clothing together. He’d determine if his current pants were salvageable after he was repaired . Tucking the bundle under his arm, he walked back into the bedroom, optics dropping to the pistol atop the chest. Even within the Bot District, he never traveled unarmed. But what good would it do him ? Limping to the bedside, he stared down at Lara. Her breathing was soft, the beat of her heart steady and slow. She’d spoken of hearts and love, and what could he know about either? Humans didn’t often use the former term literally, and the latter … Careful not to lean on his damaged leg, he bent forward and pulled the blanket over Lara’s body. She slid her arms up, taking hold of the cloth, and wrapped it around herself snugly without opening her eyes. A long, satisfied sigh escaped her before she stilled . Love . Despite his caution, he descended the steps in half the time it had taken to mount them the night before. He went to the door and didn’t allow himself any further hesitation. He didn’t succumb to the desire to go look at her again, to contemplate the serenity on her face as she slept, to listen to the soothing sound of her breathing . His internal clock had meticulously accounted for every microsecond, every minute, hour, and day, since his reactivation. It was one of his core processes, albeit a relatively simple one. Something as natural to him as breathing was to a human. Every second had always been exactly as long as the ones preceding it and following it, without differentiation, without fail . Until Lara…until she changed everything . Every minute of his journey back to Cheyenne had felt longer than a year of his life before. Each step had been an impossible distance for his damaged stride. The possibility of never seeing Lara again had altered his perception of everything . Stepping outside, he closed and locked the door behind him. The merest hint of dawn left the sky a dark, smudgy gray. He walked north, toward Warlord’s pieced- together wall . Love . He understood the word, at its most basic level. It was a deep attachment to someone. How could a seemingly simple term carry so much weight, so much depth ? To his left, leaves rustled in the persistent wind, a reminder that life always found a way — even if it needed help from time to time . Curiosity had taken root in him, that first night, and it had sprouted into fascination. Now, it was a tree tall enough to put all others in its shade. Lara was part of his existence. The simulations he ran regarding his future invariably included her, and he found himself dismissing any that didn’t — because he knew he wouldn’t be able to let her go . Despite the loathing she’d viewed him with in the beginning, Lara saw him . Not a case housing a collection of parts, not a bot…just Ronin. The change in her was clear, highlighted by her struggles against it . She’d told him she loved him. That was no small admission, those weren’t careless words of comfort. They both knew words could be cheap and meaningless, that they could be used without conviction, spoken with duplicity or outright malice. But she had proved her words through her actions . Though he couldn’t be sure — this was all too new to him, too difficult to sort out in a mind operating on logic and mathematics — he thought he loved her , too . Ronin turned the corner and proceeded east, parallel to the wall across the street. The man who’d written the attic journal had undoubtedly walked this same road, years before. The writer had loved his family, had undoubtedly been loved by them, and had lived before Warlord tore their already damaged world apart . Despite the powerful storms, the lack of resources, and the sharp contrasts of heat and cold, the Dust wasn’t the true danger. It was a force of nature, incapable of malice. People like Warlord were what Lara needed to be protected from. That was the real tragedy of this world; the Creator’s children destroying one another without thought . Amidst all that, had Ronin somehow found love ? Why else would he be so ready and willing to give up the existence he’d known for decades? He was ready to cast logic aside to pursue an emotion he shouldn’t ever have experienced . The Clinic was quiet as he turned off the main street and approached it. The gearheads on guard outside stared at him, making no comment as he passed them. Only the glass entry doors were illuminated. Inside, the place was as white and sanitized as ever, so unchanged that when Mercy — in her usual spot at the front desk — looked up at him, he wondered if he’d lapsed into a full-sensory memory. His stride was suddenly weightless, and everything moved too slowly . Mercy’s lips parted, but she didn’t smile, didn’t greet him. Following her gaze, he glanced down at his ruined torso . “Just need some reskinning,” he said . She didn’t seem to appreciate the humor .
